title: US monetary plumbing
subtitle: Fed balance sheet, reserves, TGA, reverse repo and net liquidity

series.label: Fed total assets
series.description: System Open Market Account plus lending; the size of the Fed balance sheet.
series.source_provider: FRED (Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis)
series.transform: level; converted from 'Millions of U.S. Dollars' to billions of USD

series.label: Treasuries held outright
series.description: U.S. Treasury securities held outright by the Fed (SOMA).
series.source_provider: FRED (Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis)
series.transform: level; converted from 'Millions of U.S. Dollars' to billions of USD

series.label: MBS held outright
series.description: Mortgage-backed securities held outright by the Fed (SOMA).
series.source_provider: FRED (Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis)
series.transform: level; converted from 'Millions of U.S. Dollars' to billions of USD

series.label: Reserve balances
series.description: Bank reserves held at the Fed; the core of system liquidity.
series.source_provider: FRED (Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis)
series.transform: level; converted from 'Millions of U.S. Dollars' to billions of USD

series.label: Treasury General Account
series.description: The Treasury's checking account at the Fed. Rising TGA drains reserves.
series.source_provider: FRED (Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis)
series.transform: level; converted from 'Millions of U.S. Dollars' to billions of USD

series.label: Overnight reverse repo (ON RRP)
series.description: Cash parked at the Fed overnight by money funds etc. A rising RRP drains reserves.
series.source_provider: FRED (Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis)
series.transform: level; converted from 'Billions of US Dollars' to billions of USD

series.label: Currency in circulation
series.description: Physical cash outstanding; a slow-moving drain on reserves.
series.source_provider: FRED (Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis)
series.transform: level; converted from 'Millions of U.S. Dollars' to billions of USD

net_liquidity.label: Net liquidity
net_liquidity.description: Net liquidity = Fed total assets (WALCL) minus the Treasury General Account (WTREGEN) minus overnight reverse repo (RRPONTSYD), all in billions of USD, sampled on the weekly balance-sheet dates (TGA and RRP taken at the latest value on or before each date). This is a widely-used proxy for reserves available to the financial system, not an official Fed series.

methodology: Net liquidity = Fed total assets (WALCL) minus the Treasury General Account (WTREGEN) minus overnight reverse repo (RRPONTSYD), all in billions of USD, sampled on the weekly balance-sheet dates (TGA and RRP taken at the latest value on or before each date). This is a widely-used proxy for reserves available to the financial system, not an official Fed series.
